name: Florida (United States)
description: A white field charged with a red saltire (diagonal cross). The Great Seal of Florida — circular, with a Seminole woman, a sabal palmetto, a steamboat and a rising sun — is centered over the saltire's intersection.
history: Florida's first post-Reconstruction state flag, adopted in 1868, showed the state seal on a plain white field. Governor Francis P. Fleming proposed adding the red saltire in the 1890s to prevent the flag from resembling a flag of surrender or truce when hanging limp; the diagonal cross was approved by referendum on 6 November 1900. The seal has been redesigned several times — most notably in 1985, when the Seminole woman's headdress and other details were corrected.

features.symbolism: the red saltire (Cross of Burgundy) was added in 1900 to evoke Florida's Spanish heritage and to keep the flag from looking like a flag of truce when limp

depicts a Seminole woman scattering hibiscus flowers, a steamboat, a sabal palmetto (the state tree) and the sun's rays over a coastline; encircled by the words 'GREAT SEAL OF THE STATE OF FLORIDA' and 'IN GOD WE TRUST'
